mechanism: Turbulence broadband noise from bulk tidal flow through channel geometry

puget_sound_geology.origin: Cordilleran Ice Sheet, last glacial maximum ~17,000 years ago

tidal_mechanics.pattern: Mixed semidiurnal: 2 highs + 2 lows per day, unequal heights

acoustic_physics.primary_mechanism: Turbulence from bulk flow past irregular channel geometry
acoustic_physics.human_perception: Felt more than heard. Subsonic pulse. Body-felt at major current locations.
acoustic_physics.e2_placement: Lowest energy in the SPS tidal subseries. Background level -- not dominant, not dramatic. The foundation beneath all biological sound in the Sound.
acoustic_physics.comparison_to_252: 252 (cobble beach, E=4) is audible at 200m. 253 (tidal current) is subsonic and diffuse -- the entire water column.

underwater_acoustic_environment.tide_rips: Convergence zones where opposing currents meet. Audible surface turbulence. Bird feeding aggregations.

cultural_context.navigation_use: Tidal currents as free transportation for canoe travel. Tidal phase timing essential.
cultural_context.fishing_use: Current mapped forage fish (herring, smelt, anchovy) at convergence zones and sills
cultural_context.monitoring: NOAA CO-OPS current stations at Tacoma Narrows, Admiralty Inlet, Deception Pass

arc_position.preceding_arc: 245-252 abiotic natural sounds (waterfalls, rain, wind, tidal surge)
arc_position.current_arc: 253-255 infrastructure/maritime arc (tidal current, fog horn, log truck)

theory_nodes.concept: Tidal prism: 25 cubic kilometers entering and exiting Puget Sound per tidal cycle via Admiralty Inlet. ~10x volume of Lake Washington. The scale of the tidal exchange is the entry's primary datum: this is the largest single physical process in the Sound, and it generates E=2 -- the lowest energy rating in the SPS series. Volume does not equal acoustic energy.
theory_nodes.description: 25 km³ tidal prism: maximum volume, minimum acoustic energy (E=2).

theory_nodes.concept: The acoustic character of tidal current is infrasound: below 20-100 Hz, felt more than heard. This contrasts with the tidal surge on cobble (degree 252, E=4) which is fully audible. The 252-253 pair documents two acoustic expressions of the same tidal system: the noisy shore interface (audible) and the quiet bulk flow (infrasonic).
theory_nodes.description: Infrasound: tidal current below human hearing threshold.

cross_references.relationship: 252 is the shore interface (audible, E=4). 253 is the bulk flow (infrasonic, E=2). Two acoustic expressions of the same tidal system.
Both at Admiralty Inlet. The tidal current flows through the inlet; the fog horn signals across it. Natural hydraulics and human maritime safety infrastructure at the same location.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=7 sludge vs E=2 tidal current. Both: low-frequency, mass-based, measured in bulk rather than velocity. The slow avalanche of sludge riffs and the slow movement of 25 billion cubic meters. The asymmetry in E rating (7 vs 2) reflects acoustic reality: the tidal current is physically enormous but acoustically quiet; sludge is physically smaller but acoustically massive.

milestones: TIDAL CURRENT (bulk flow) documented alongside TIDAL SURGE (shore interface, 252)
